About.Name: Eliab Alula
About.AboutText: I am a Computer Science student at Seattle Pacific University with an interest in software development and problem-solving. Through my coursework and personal projects, I have gained experience in C++, data structures, and programming fundamentals. I enjoy building programs that help me improve my technical skills while continuing to learn more about how software is designed and developed.

Experience.ExperienceText:
I am currently a Computer Science student building a strong foundation in programming and problem solving. Through my coursework, I have worked with C++ to develop projects that focus on data structures, memory management, and algorithmic thinking. I am actively improving my skills by writing clean, efficient code and learning how to debug and test programs effectively.

I am focused on continuing to grow as a developer by gaining hands-on experience and applying what I learn to real-world projects.
Experience.EducationText: My studies focus on core computer science concepts such as data structures, algorithms, and software development. I have completed coursework in problem solving and programming as well as multiple data structures courses, where I developed projects using C++ and strengthened my understanding of how software is built and optimized.
Experience.EmploymentText: I am currently focused on my academic work and personal projects to build a strong technical foundation. I regularly practice coding, work on programming assignments, and develop projects that improve my understanding of software development and problem solving.
Experience.SkillsText: I have experience working with C++ to build programs that involve data structures and memory management. I am comfortable using development tools such as Git and modern IDEs, and I am continuously improving my problem-solving and debugging skills through practice and coursework.

Project1.Title: Polynomial Class (C++)
Project1.Desc: Built a polynomial class in C++ that supports basic mathematical operations using dynamic memory and structured programming.

Project2.Title: Typing Speed Test
Project2.Desc: Developed an interactive C++ typing speed test that measures words per minute, typing accuracy, and completion time using timers and string processing.

Project3.Title: Student Grade Management System
Project3.Desc: Developed a C++ application to manage and store student records using files.

Project4.Title: Task Manager
Project4.Desc: C++ application that's able to manage tasks with features such as adding, updating, deleting, sorting, and saving records.
